common.explore_convergence: Explore the convergence between quantum computing and artificial intelligence through interactive simulators, QNLP, and variational optimizers.

home.studio_desc: Design complex quantum circuits with intuitive drag-and-drop.
home.algorithms_desc: Shor, Grover, VQE, QAOA and more ready for execution and learning.
home.qnlp_desc: Combine natural language with quantum mechanics for semantic analysis.
home.grover_insight: Grover speeds up searches from O(N) to O(√N), finding |{{key}}⟩ exponentially faster.
home.shor_desc: Capable of factoring integers exponentially faster than any known classical algorithm.

execution.load_circuit_desc: Navigate to Quantum Studio to build or modify circuits before running here.
execution.about_desc: Monitor circuit execution with real-time performance metrics and state visualization.

studio.title: Quantum Computer Studio
studio.no_results: No execution results to display. Run the circuit to view metrics.
studio.js_snippet_desc: Use this code in your Node.js or browser project to run this exact circuit.
studio.qasm_editor_desc: Write QASM 3.1 code to update the visual circuit in real-time.
studio.qiskit_snippet_desc: Use this Python script to run your exact circuit on IBM Quantum hardware or local Aer simulators.
studio.cirq_snippet_desc: Use this Python script to run your exact circuit using Google Quantum AI Cirq framework.
studio.braket_snippet_desc: Use this Python script to deploy your circuit via AWS Braket SDK (Local Simulator or QPUs like IonQ/Rigetti).
studio.penny_snippet_desc: Use this Python script to build Quantum Machine Learning (QML) layers using Xanadu PennyLane framework.
studio.qsharp_snippet_desc: Use this Q# script to run your exact circuit via the Microsoft Quantum Development Kit (QDK) or Azure Quantum.

qnlp.title: Quantum NLP (QNLP)
qnlp.semantic_desc: Type a phrase to convert it into a quantum circuit
qnlp.placeholder: e.g. cat happy, cat chases mouse hungry
qnlp.results_desc: The sentiment is derived from the final quantum state of the circuit representation.

syntax.title: Quantum Syntax Tree
syntax.empty_phrase: Type a phrase to see its syntactic structure.
syntax.interpretation_desc: Nouns occupy quantum wires (states). Verbs and adjectives act as quantum operators causing entanglement or phase rotations between subjects.

qnlp.how_it_works_desc1: QNLP maps language structures (nouns, verbs) to quantum objects (states, gates).
qnlp.how_it_works_desc2: This demo uses a simplified DisCoCat (Distributional Compositional Categorical) model where:
qnlp.how_it_works_desc3: The final "sentiment" is measured by the probability of the system collapsing into a base state.

algorithms.title: Quantum Algorithms
algorithms.library_desc: Select an algorithm to run and visualize
algorithms.select_to_view: Select an algorithm to view details and complexity analysis
algorithms.about_desc1: Quantum algorithms leverage superposition and entanglement to solve problems exponentially faster than classical computers for specific tasks.
algorithms.specialized_oracles_desc: Modular logic for function property identification.
algorithms.specialized_qml_desc: Variational circuits for chemistry and optimization.
algorithms.specialized_qnlp_desc: Semantic reasoning using quantum syntax trees.

items.grover: Grover's algorithm is a quantum search algorithm that finds a target item in an unstructured database in O(√N) queries. Classical algorithms require O(N) queries.
items.deutsch-jozsa: The Deutsch-Jozsa algorithm determines if a function is constant or balanced with just one query, demonstrating quantum advantage over classical computing.
items.shor: Shor's algorithm is designed to factor large integers exponentially faster than classical algorithms, with implications for cryptography.
items.qft: Quantum Fourier Transform (QFT) is a linear transformation that takes a quantum state and converts it into the frequency domain, fundamental to many algorithms.
items.qpe: Quantum Phase Estimation is used to estimate the eigenvalue of a unitary operator, crucial for algorithms like Shor's and HHL.
items.bernstein-vazirani: The Bernstein-Vazirani algorithm identifies a hidden string using a single query, demonstrating exponential speedup over classical methods for oracle problems.
items.simon: Simon's algorithm solves the hidden subgroup problem efficiently, providing exponential advantage for finding periodic functions and inspiring Shor's algorithm.
items.qaoa: Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm is a hybrid classical-quantum algorithm for solving combinatorial optimization problems using variational methods.
items.vqe: Variational Quantum Eigensolver is a quantum-classical hybrid algorithm for finding the ground state energy of molecular systems using quantum circuits.

datalab.title: Data Lab
datalab.column_selection_warning: Select at least one column to visualize
datalab.quantum_integration_desc: Map your normalized data to quantum states and run computations.
datalab.amplitude_desc: Amplitude encoding will create superposition states with {{count}} amplitudes
datalab.angle_desc: Angle encoding will create rotation gates with {{count}} angles
datalab.step1: 1. Upload a CSV/TSV file or load a sample dataset
datalab.about_desc: The Data Lab allows you to convert classical data into quantum states, enabling quantum machine learning and data processing.

stateviewer.title: State Viewer
stateviewer.desc: Visualize amplitudes, phases, and probabilities for any circuit or algorithm. Use "View Current Circuit" to load from Studio.
stateviewer.detailed_desc: Comprehensive state analysis with full metrics
stateviewer.quick_algorithms_desc: Inject preset states into the observer
stateviewer.about_desc: The State Viewer provides a non-destructive window into the current quantum state vector before measurement.

visual_lab.desc: Interactive visualization of quantum state behavior.
visual_lab.super_desc: The qubit exists in both |0⟩ and |1⟩ simultaneously. It is not "between" them, but rather a "cloud of probability" until measured.
visual_lab.zero_desc: The qubit is strictly in state |0⟩. If measured, it will yield "0" with 100% probability.
visual_lab.one_desc: The qubit is strictly in state |1⟩. If measured, it will yield "1" with 100% probability.
visual_lab.collapse_desc: The observation forces the system into a single classical state.
visual_lab.tip1: Apply X to flip from 0 to 1.
visual_lab.tip2: Apply H to enter superposition (the cloud).
visual_lab.tip3: Wait for the cloud to pulse, then click Measure.
visual_lab.tip4: Observe the "Snap" as it collapses to a single point.

api.title: Quantum API
api.subtitle: Interactive REPL for quantum circuit programming
api.no_circuit_found: No circuit found in code. Make sure you have a "const circuit = {...}" declaration.

qml.title: QML Hub
qml.subtitle: Variational Algorithms
qml.desc: Explore Quantum Machine Learning and Variational Optimization (NISQ-Era computing).
qml.chemistry_advantage: VQE algorithms bypass the exponential Hilbert space memory requirement of classical diagonalization (Full CI).
qml.optimization_advantage: QAOA provides a heuristic pathway to solve combinatorial optimization problems traditionally hard for classical simulated annealing.

oracles.title: Quantum Oracles
oracles.desc: Oracles are black-box functions used in algorithms like Deutsch–Jozsa, Grover, and Bernstein-Vazirani.
oracles.about_desc: The Deutsch–Jozsa algorithm determines if an oracle is constant or balanced in just one query, while classical computers need 2ⁿ queries.

gates_lib.title: Gates Library
gates_lib.click_for_details: Click a gate to see details
